title: Personalize Agents with Custom Variables
lede: Feed your Smart Agent live context - a caller's name, address, and any custom fields - so it can greet people by name and tailor every conversation.

steps.title: Insert Variables in the Prompt
steps.content:
1. Go to Intelligence Center → Smart Agents and open your agent.
  2. Click into a text field such as the system prompt or the first message.
  3. Type an opening brace { to open the variable menu. Built-in variables include {first_name}, {last_name}, {email}, and {full_address}.
  4. Pick one to insert it at the cursor - at call time it is replaced with the real value.

images.caption: Type { in a prompt field to insert CRM variables.
Type { in a prompt field to insert CRM variables.

steps.title: Manage Templates & Variables
steps.content:
1. Open the Templates & Variables panel in the editor.
  2. Here you can save reusable prompt templates and manage the variables your agent knows about.

images.caption: Templates & Variables lives in the middle column of the editor.
Templates & Variables lives in the middle column of the editor.

steps.title: Add Custom Data to a Lead or Contact
steps.content:
1. Open a Lead or Contact in the CRM.
  2. Add custom fields (a name and a value) to that record.
  3. Those values become available to the agent as context whenever it speaks with that person.

steps.title: Send Variables via the API
steps.content:
1. When you start a call through the API, include a customVars object in the request metadata with your key/value pairs.
  2. This is ideal for passing context from an external CRM or app at call time.
  3. The agent receives those values and can reference them in the conversation.
